The discussions on Digital India also involved other ministries, including Minority Affairs, Urban Development, Environment, Forests and Climate Change, and Finance. The Digital India policy was initiated in 2015 with an agreement signed by the Ministry of Communications and Information Technology. The focus of the policy was on digital infrastructure, digital services, and digital literacy. The Digital India campaign was launched in February 2016, and various ministries started implementing the policy in their respective areas. The Ministry of Communications and Information Technology expanded the Digital India scheme in August 2015, and the Ministry of Environment, Forests and Climate Change initiated a Digital India initiative in December 2015. Privacy concerns were raised in December 2015.

In 2016, the Ministry of Urban Development started a Digital India project, and the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology outlined the Digital India plan and job creation under the program. The Ministry of Finance also started a Digital India project. The Ministry of Communications and Information Technology held discussions on privacy concerns.

In 2017, the Ministry of Communications started working on the Digital India programme, and the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology continued its efforts with multiple announcements. The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology also made a significant announcement in January 2018, reiterating its commitment to the Digital India programme.

Additionally, the Ministry of Minority Affairs has been involved in skill development under Make in India and Digital India. The Ministry of Urban Development has been responsible for the Digital India Project. The Ministry of Environment, Forests and Climate Change has initiated the Digital India Initiative. The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology has been involved in promoting agriculture under Digital India, executing works under the programme, and implementing the Digital India Plan and Job Creation under Digital India. The Ministry of Finance has also contributed to the Digital India Project.
